location.official_address: Tanjore Main Road / Trichy-Thanjavur Highway, National Institute of Technology, Tiruchirappalli - 620015, Tamil Nadu, India
location.nearest_major_hospital: BHEL Hospital (official referral hospital for specialised care)
location.technology_ecosystem: Large residential institute campus; local technology/start-up ecosystem not quantitatively verified
location.data_origin: Official institution/JoSAA profile; distance conflict flagged

senior_junior_culture.summary: Public evidence is too limited for a confident generalisation. Officially, first-year students have structured clubs, NSS/NCC/NSO and hostel governance; student-source coverage did not provide enough independent, specific accounts of senior-junior interaction.
positive_themes: clubs and fests create cross-year interaction opportunities
negative_themes: insufficient direct evidence; isolated online comments should not be generalized

academic_culture.summary: Institution sources show a broad technical curriculum and strong research/industry ecosystem. Student reports indicate that workload and CGPA difficulty can vary materially by branch, with EEE frequently described as demanding.
negative_themes: academic pressure reported by some students

hostel_reality.summary: Officially, the institute has a very large residential system with 22 boys and 6 girls hostels and capacity for 6,966 residents. Student reports are mixed: first-year hostels and connectivity receive some positive mentions, while heat, variable amenities and average mess quality recur.
positive_themes: some reports of good first-year hostels and fast hostel internet
negative_themes: amenity quality may vary by hostel/year
mess quality described as average or variable
hostel_reality.evidence_strength: moderate for broad themes; anecdotal for quality claims

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official evidence is strong that NITT has a broad club ecosystem and major student-run fests. Student sources generally describe fests and campus community positively, although some accounts criticise restrictive rules or internal culture.
positive_themes: many technical, cultural and social clubs
negative_themes: club activity intensity and recruitment selectivity not systematically verified
some negative anecdotes about fest/team culture
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: strong official evidence; mixed student sentiment

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement outcomes and the presence of technical groups such as Delta Force and Spider R&D support a strong technical/placement environment. Company eligibility by branch and the day-to-day coding culture were not comprehensively verified.
positive_themes: high official placement percentages in several branches
negative_themes: role and branch eligibility are not published in the brochure

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is outside central Tiruchirappalli and functions as a large residential environment. Students often mention needing a cycle for internal movement, limited immediately adjacent off-campus options and very hot weather.

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available qualitative accounts range from highly positive alumni memories to sharp criticism of weather, hostels and gender-differentiated restrictions. The evidence is not representative enough for a numeric satisfaction rating.

verified_strengths: Top-10 NIRF engineering position for three consecutive years (rank 9 in 2023-2025)
Institution of National Importance with 800-acre residential campus
Strong official placement percentages and NIRF median salary data
Substantial tuition remission for eligible income/category groups
watch_items: Hostel annual cost and latest placement packages need verification
Campus is outside city centre and climate is hot
Recent student reports raise gender-curfew concerns
parent_mode_summary.advice: Compare branch fit and official category/quota cutoffs first; treat placement percentages only with their stated denominator.

best_for: Students seeking a strong national peer group, technical clubs and major fests
Students comfortable with a large residential campus and branch-specific academic intensity
consider_carefully: Students prioritising unrestricted late-night campus freedom, especially women
Students strongly preferring city-centre access or mild weather
Students hoping to change branch after first year
student_mode_summary.advice: Choose the branch you are willing to complete, verify current hostel rules, and speak to current students from the same branch and gender/hostel context.

decision_risks.detail: Use 2023-2025 history only; do not represent it as a guaranteed 2026 outcome.

decision_risks.risk: Placement statistics use different denominators and scopes
decision_risks.detail: T&P percentages are for registered students; NIRF uses graduating students and reports placed/higher studies separately.

decision_risks.risk: Latest final placement packages and 2024-25 outcomes are missing
decision_risks.detail: Recruiter brochure is not a final audited outcome report.

decision_risks.risk: Hostel and mess annual cost is incomplete
decision_risks.detail: Only first odd-semester 2026-27 hostel fee is available in the extracted source.
Recent online accounts are consistent enough to warrant direct verification, but remain anecdotal and rules can change.

decision_risks.risk: Hot climate and campus distance from city
decision_risks.detail: Official campus is outside central Trichy; student reports repeatedly mention heat.
JoSAA profile explicitly states NIT Tiruchirappalli has no branch-change policy after admission.

data_gaps: Current NAAC status/grade was not verified from an authoritative current certificate.
Complete programme-wise NBA accreditation roster was not found; only Mechanical Engineering 2023-2028 was verified.
2024-25 and 2025-26 final placement statistics, average/highest packages, PPO and internship counts were not officially available in a consolidated final report.
Recruiter roles, eligible branches, selected counts and packages are absent from the official brochure.
Full-year 2026-27 hostel and mess cost is unavailable from the extracted odd-semester circular.
Exact current airport/hospital distances and geocoordinates were not map-verified.
Current detailed eligibility/benefit PDFs for RECT 1989 and RECT 1978 EWS calls were not extracted.
Club recruitment processes and latest activity dates need direct manual verification.
Student-experience evidence is self-selected and not statistically representative.

conflicts.possible_reason: Different denominator, time of reporting, programme coverage and offers-vs-students scope
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Do not merge; request final audited placement report with denominator definitions
